comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Plan-review (this run): signed PASS, 0 blocking, 1 minor advisory (P6 ac-quality: some ACs bundle deliverables with 'and'), plus informative indeterminates. Disposition (not editing AC text, to preserve the fresh PASS attestation; honoring intent in the held-out oracle + implementation instead): (P6) tests are split one-concern-per-function; (E6/F1) AC5's docs claim will be genuinely proven by an oracle test asserting docs/event-schema.md + docs/output-schemas.md contain all six enum values + the inference marker (test_schema_coverage.py does NOT check docs today); (T1/T3) gen_types._pytype has no const handler -> will add const->Literal[True] so creation_channel_inferred renders correctly and gen_types --check passes; (A1/T8) create_core validator accepts all six enum values but REJECTS 'unknown' and out-of-enum for live writes (AC4), and creation_channel_inferred has zero writers this story (recorded+unknown projections omit it); (G1G2) ensure_identity_for default 'python' would mis-attribute the apply_inbound_records Jira ghost-mint call site -> flagged for e622 to override to jira at that boundary. Proceeding to claim + held-out TDD.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

Auditors need an explicit genesis channel for the three local public interfaces. CLI, MCP, and Python currently converge on shared create functions, so inferring the caller inside the core would collapse distinct paths. This story establishes the additive contract and delivers the first complete vertical slice from entry boundary through CREATE storage, reducer state, typed output, and facade tests.

## What

- Add the canonical creation-channel enum to src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json with exactly cli, mcp, python, jira, import, unknown. Extend src/rebar/schemas/gen_types.py so JSON Schema {"const": true} generates Literal[True], then regenerate src/rebar/types.py through the existing schema generator.
- Add the matching runtime constant plus validate_creation_channel(...) helper to src/rebar/reducer/_version.py and a contract test that prevents runtime/schema vocabulary drift. create_core calls this helper, so every local live writer rejects unknown or off-vocabulary values. The Jira inbound CREATE seam bypasses create_core (inbound_translate writes raw event files directly) and is OUT of scope here; the dependent Jira/import story e622 wires that seam to the same helper.
- Make composer.create_core require an internal keyword-only creation_channel with no default, so omission raises TypeError at the internal seam, and validate it before writing. Every existing caller is updated in this story. The public rebar.create_ticket, rebar.idea, rebar.create_identity, and session-log library helpers accept an internal creation_channel keyword defaulting to python; this temporarily gives NDJSON import python without breaking the 6fe2 delivery boundary, and e622 explicitly overrides it with import. CLI handlers pass cli; MCP create_ticket, create_idea, create_identity, and log_session pass mcp. Thread the value explicitly rather than inspecting the call stack or introducing global context.
- Add creation_channel="python" keyword parameters to create_identity_core and ensure_identity_for. Local library callers use that default; identity_cli passes cli. This story owns those shared signatures so the dependent Jira story only supplies jira at the inbound call boundary.
- Store creation_channel in CREATE.data for every new local creation and project it into compiled state exactly once. Define _IMMUTABLE_EDIT_FIELDS = frozenset({"creation_channel", "creation_channel_inferred"}) in reducer/_processors.py; process_edit skips those names before its generic assignment, while specialized processors never assign them. A legacy CREATE with no value provisionally projects creation_channel=unknown without creation_channel_inferred. Child e622 treats absent/None/unknown-without-marker as unresolved legacy input so positive Jira inference can replace this provisional fallback.
- Add optional public schema fields creation_channel and creation_channel_inferred to ticket_state.schema.json. Encode the marker as {"const": true}; recorded and unknown projections omit it. Regenerate derived public types and confirm show, list, search, and export projections retain the field through their existing TicketState shape.
- Cover standard tickets plus idea, identity, and session_log creation paths. Internal automation that directly calls the public library is truthfully python.

## Approach Rationale

Explicit boundary threading is chosen because the interface knows the channel and the shared core does not. It is simpler and more testable than stack inspection or context-local state, and it follows the existing create_core/source_* pattern for additive CREATE metadata. A closed enum keeps audit results comparable; unknown is reserved for historical compatibility and is rejected for new live writes.

## Scope

IN: local CLI/MCP/Python writers, helper creation flows, reducer projection, canonical output schema, generated types, facade/contract tests, and local-interface documentation.

OUT: Jira inbound creation, NDJSON import, historical Jira inference, SNAPSHOT durability, caller-selectable free-form values, list filtering, and security attestation. Those are assigned to later children or explicitly excluded by the epic.

## Files

- src/rebar/_commands/composer.py: validate and write explicit channel; CLI create passes cli.
- src/rebar/_lib_writes.py: Python defaults and internal MCP override plumbing for public creation helpers.
- src/rebar/_mcp_writes.py: MCP boundary attribution.
- src/rebar/_commands/idea.py and session_log.py: explicit helper attribution.
- src/rebar/_commands/identity.py: own create_identity_core/ensure_identity_for channel signatures, Python defaults, and identity_cli attribution.
- src/rebar/reducer/_version.py and reducer/_processors.py: canonical runtime vocabulary and state projection.
- src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json and ticket_state.schema.json: canonical public contract.
- src/rebar/schemas/gen_types.py: render JSON Schema const values as Literal[...] before generated-type checks.
- src/rebar/types.py: generated output only via python -m rebar.schemas.gen_types.
- tests/interfaces/facades/test_creation_channel.py and schema contract tests.
- docs/event-schema.md and docs/output-schemas.md: local channel contract.

## Dependencies

None. This is the foundation for the Jira/import and compaction stories.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] CLI rebar create and idea/session-log/identity creation persist creation_channel=cli in CREATE.data and return cli through show/list state. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/interfaces/facades/test_creation_channel.py -k cli.
- [ ] MCP create_ticket, create_idea, create_identity, and first-use log_session persist and return creation_channel=mcp. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/interfaces/facades/test_creation_channel.py -k mcp.
- [ ] Direct Python create_ticket, idea, create_identity, append_session_log, and start_session_log persist and return creation_channel=python, including internal automation that uses those public functions. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/interfaces/facades/test_creation_channel.py -k python.
- [ ] New local writers reject unknown or values outside the closed enum. A legacy CREATE with no channel provisionally reduces to unknown without creation_channel_inferred, remaining eligible for e622 inference. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/interfaces/facades/test_creation_channel.py -k "invalid or legacy".
- [ ] An EDIT.fields payload containing creation_channel or creation_channel_inferred is skipped by the _IMMUTABLE_EDIT_FIELDS guard in process_edit; the STATUS, LINK, COMMENT, and TAG_DELTA processors likewise never assign genesis provenance, so the CREATE value is immutable after genesis. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/interfaces/facades/test_creation_channel.py -k immutable.
- [ ] ticket_state JSON Schema, generated TicketState types, runtime show/list/search/export outputs, plus docs expose the same six-value enum and true-only optional inference marker; gen_types renders const true as Literal[True]. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/interfaces/facades/test_creation_channel.py -k projections && uv run pytest -q tests/interfaces/contracts/test_schema_ticket_state.py tests/interfaces/contracts/test_schema_outputs.py tests/interfaces/contracts/test_schema_coverage.py && python -m rebar.schemas.gen_types --check.
- [ ] Existing creation behavior, error semantics, and event canonicalization remain unchanged apart from the additive keys. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/interfaces/store/test_canonical_event_bytes.py tests/interfaces/facades/test_library.py tests/interfaces/facades/test_cli.py tests/interfaces/facades/test_mcp.py.

file_impact.reason: require, validate, and persist local creation channel
direct Python defaults and internal facade threading
own create_identity_core/ensure_identity_for channel signatures, Python defaults, and identity_cli attribution
canonical runtime channel vocabulary and shared writer validator
CREATE projection, provisional legacy default, and explicit EDIT immutability guard
render JSON Schema const values as Literal[...]

compiled_state.title: Expose creation_channel for CLI, MCP, and Python ingress

TicketState schema and generated type contract remain aligned
